methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Last Energy, a Series C nuclear energy company founded by Bret Kugelmass, is valued within a sector comprising three public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and eight private peers including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

A peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with sufficient depth—no narrow peer subset met the three-datapoint threshold necessary for reliability. Consequently, the valuation relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to Last Energy's $100M total raised proxy, yielding $170M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $144M to $195M, with a median point of $170M. This range reflects reasonable bounds around the stage-progression output, though the single-method foundation represents a material constraint. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 1.7x multiplier to Last Energy's specific unit economics and technology maturity relative to the broader nuclear sector, and the absence of narrowly-comparable private financing data to cross-validate the stage-based approach. Additional datapoints from recent Series B or Series D rounds within advanced nuclear would improve confidence in the range.
